concerns.text: FR-001 mandates 'preserving semantic content' but provides no verifiable metric or procedure to confirm this. 'Semantic content' is subjective without a defined validation step (e.g., 'verified by object detection model X with >95% IoU overlap' or 'human coder agreement >80%'). Without this, the requirement is untestable.

FR-007 requires excluding 'straight-lining' participants but fails to define the detection threshold. 'Identical ratings' is ambiguous (e.g., 50% identical? 100% identical? 10 consecutive identical?). A verifiable FR must specify the exact rule (e.g., 'exclude if 100% of ratings are identical' or 'exclude if variance < 0.1'). Currently, implementers cannot determine when the condition is met.

The Assumptions section contains a malformed sentence: 'The The system MUST identify...'. This appears to be a copy-paste error where a requirement was pasted into the assumptions list. While the content describes a process, its placement in 'Assumptions' makes it unenforceable as a requirement. It must be moved to Functional Requirements (e.g., FR-008) with a verifiable success criterion (e.g., '≥80% inter-rater reliability measured by Cohen's κ'). As written, it is neither a clear assumption nor a testable requirement.

SC-005 states the CI width is 'measured against a predefined precision threshold' but does not specify what that threshold is or where it is defined. In a planning spec, if a threshold is deferred, the SC must explicitly state 'deferred' or reference a specific document/section where the threshold will be defined. Without this, the criterion is unmeasurable.
